decisive_findings: Side A's final artifact contains a major structural defect: the four required sections (Decision Frame, Unknowns & Evidence Gaps, Pass/Fail Readiness, Recommended Next Artifact) are duplicated — appearing once in the main body and again as stubs at the bottom — undermining internal consistency and board readability.

Side B provides concrete, actionable specifics a board can act on: a 6-week timeline, $40-60K cost estimate, named gap-closing actions with turnaround times, and specific deliverables. Side A's recommendation remains abstract ('bounded evidence-generation phase') with no timeline, cost, or concrete actions.

Both sides responded well to critique, but Side B's revision was surgically precise — reframing criteria provenance and fixing the none-vs-partial inconsistency while preserving all substantive content. Side A's revision, while honest about evidentiary scarcity, hollowed out the artifact to the point of limited board utility.

Side B's illustrative scenario details (38% ARR concentration, 15-20% margin risk, Van Westendorp methodology) are fabricated but serve as a concrete template a real team could populate, making the artifact immediately useful as a working document. Side A's abstract framing requires a full rewrite before it could be used.
rationale: Side B is clearly stronger across decision usefulness, internal consistency, and claim quality. Its final artifact reads as a board-ready document with concrete actions, timelines, costs, and structured pass/fail criteria that a real board could challenge and act upon. Side A, while admirably honest about evidentiary scarcity after absorbing critique, produced a final artifact with duplicated sections and abstract recommendations that would leave a board without enough specificity to approve or reject anything. Both sides showed strong critique responsiveness, and both face the same evidence discipline constraint (no real data was provided in the case packet), but Side B turned that constraint into an advantage by constructing a realistic illustrative framework rather than retreating to pure abstraction.
